summary: This course will mainly focus on improving students' intelligibility in spoken English. Students will learn how English is spoken in natural settings and how the sounds of the English language are transcribed using International Phonetic Alphabets. They will also be introduced to the basics of phonetics; the articulation of the vowel and consonant sounds and some features of connected speech.
aims: Students will learn English phonetics theoretically and practically. The aim is not only to accumulate knowledge of English phonetics but also to be able to pronounce it correctly.
goals: Students can understand and pronounce English phonetic symbols correctly.
Students can read aloud elementary, junior high, and high school English textbooks with correct pronunciation.

textbooks: All the materials will be provided by the tutor.
prereq: This course is intended for students who plan to become a teacher of English in Japan. All teaching in this course is carried out in English. Students whose first language is not English are expected to start the course with a minimum CEFR level of B2 (equivalent IELTS 5.5, Eiken Pre-1, TOEIC L&R 785, TOEFL iBT 72). If you are not sure if you meet the prerequisite, please contact the tutor for advice.
prep: Students are required to work very hard outside the regular class hours practising speaking.
